title: memory leak in `podman build --layers` when using ADD from network
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Trying to build with --layers seems to cause a memory leak when using ADD from the network (i.e. `ADD http://`). If I use add from local then I don't have the problem. Here is the Dockerfile:

```
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:27

LABEL RUN sudo podman run -it -v $PWD/logs/:/home/user/.weechat/logs/:z --name weechat weechat

RUN dnf install -y weechat python2-websocket-client

ENV HOME /home/user
RUN useradd -m user

RUN mkdir -p /home/user/.weechat/python/autoload/
ADD https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wee-slack/wee-slack/v2.0.0/wee_slack.py /home/user/.weechat/python/autoload/wee_slack.py
ADD start-script.txt /home/user/start-script.txt

RUN chown -R user $HOME

VOLUME /home/user/.weechat/logs/

WORKDIR $HOME
USER user

ENV TZ='America/New_York'

CMD weechat -r "$(cat start-script.txt  | grep -v '^#' | tr '\n' ';')"
```

Here is the output:


```
  PID USER      PR  NI    VIRT    RES    SHR S  %CPU %MEM     TIME+ COMMAND                                                                                                                                                                                                    
 1838 root      20   0 12.019g 8.864g  24032 S 188.0 56.9   1:07.56 podman
```

comments.body:
@dustymabe thanks for the problem report.  Just to make sure I'm on the same page, if you change this line in the Dockerfile: 

```
ADD https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wee-slack/wee-slack/v2.0.0/wee_slack.py /home/user/.weechat/python/autoload/wee_slack.py
```
to
```
ADD ./wee_slack.py /home/user/.weechat/python/autoload/wee_slack.py
```
it works? Did you by chance try doing the bud command without the `--layers` param?  Did that work?

> it works?

correct

>  Did you by chance try doing the bud command without the --layers param? Did that work?

just tried without `--layers`. I do not see this bug without `--layers`.
Thanks for the follow up @dustymabe, that will help narrow the search down.
@dustymabe I just tried this out with the latest podman and it worked for me.  Could you attempt this again? with podman 0.8.3 or later?

just tried with `podman-0.8.3-1.git9d09a4d.fc28.x86_64` rpm and I got a segfault

```
[vagrant@vanilla-f28 weechat]$ bash build.sh 
+ sudo podman build -t weechat --layers ./
STEP 1: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:6b8551f02cfd71ef7b00fc994a1c573c53409552504fc0edd4cc14164e2e40d9
 85.23 MB / 85.23 MB [======================================================] 5s
Copying config sha256:e555121ced0fcad9197d7d0445daff0e42d8f0e0c37362b66b817b8713dcbb3a
 1.27 KB / 1.27 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: LABEL RUN sudo podman run -it -v $PWD/logs/:/home/user/.weechat/logs/:z --name weechat weechat
--> ffd8bfcff2f7daf20c49ed57f69bd9ab1358a6e70610b9f5cdf8d5d525f604de
STEP 3: FROM ffd8bfcff2f7daf20c49ed57f69bd9ab1358a6e70610b9f5cdf8d5d525f604de
STEP 4: RUN dnf install -y weechat python2-websocket-client
container exited on segmentation fault
error building at step {Env:[DISTTAG=f28container FGC=f28 PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[dnf install -y weechat python2-websocket-client] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN dnf install -y weechat python2-websocket-client Original:RUN dnf install -y weechat python2-websocket-client}: exit status 1
```

Note I ran `sudo restorecon -R -v /var/lib/containers` to make sure I wasn't hitting any issues like https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1118#issuecomment-411388062
@rhatdan @dustymabe just tried it with buildah 1.4-dev and podman 0.8.2.1 and the build works fine for me also. Tried it with the exact Dockerfile provided in this issue.

> @rhatdan @dustymabe just tried it with buildah 1.4-dev and podman 0.8.2.1 and the build works fine for me also. Tried it with the exact Dockerfile provided in this issue.

can you try with the stable versions podman in Fedora 28 ? Also note that the build works fine on first run but subsequent runs that try to use a cached layer fails with the OOM.
@dustymabe thanks for the info. I found the issue, opening a PR with the fix.
Being fixed in https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/947, will vendor into podman once it is merged into buildah.
